description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted approval to larotrectinib for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients with solid tumors that: have a neurotrophic receptor tyrosine kinase (NTRK) gene fusion without a known acquired resistance mutation, are metastatic or where surgical resection is likely to result in severe morbidity, and have no satisfactory alternative treatments or that have progressed following treatment. Larotrectinib's product label specifies to select patients for therapy based on an FDA-approved test.

contributor.name: Van Allen lab
contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

contributions.description: The FDA provided regular approval to larotrectinib for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients with solid tumors that: have a neurotrophic receptor tyrosine kinase (NTRK) gene fusion without a known acquired resistance mutation, are metastatic or where surgical resection is likely to result in severe morbidity, and have no satisfactory alternative treatments or that have progressed following treatment.

indication.indication: VITRAKVI is a kinase inhibitor indicated for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients with solid tumors that: have a neurotrophic receptor tyrosine kinase (NTRK) gene fusion without a known acquired resistance mutation, are metastatic or where surgical resection is likely to result in severe morbidity, and have no satisfactory alternative treatments or that have progressed following treatment. Select patients for therapy based on an FDA-approved test.
